items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering amnesty to businesses that may not have proper licenses, with targeted outreach to shopping centers. This could affect how the city enforces business regulations and help previously non-compliant shops get legitimate.

items.headline: New economic development incentives for retail and startups announced
items.summary: The city is introducing new incentive programs designed to attract and support retail businesses and startups in Cupertino. This could change the local business landscape and attract new companies to the area.

items.headline: Mixed-use residential development approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council moved forward on a second reading for a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road, including a development agreement. This project will add housing and likely commercial space to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4), which provides state funds to cities that streamline housing approvals. If awarded, this could accelerate housing projects in Mountain View.

items.headline: City seeks public input on federal housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is taking comments on how to spend HUD federal housing funds for fiscal year 2026/27. This determines what affordable housing and community programs the city will fund with the money.

items.headline: City holds community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program likely addresses vehicle dwelling and homelessness, suggesting the city is exploring new approaches to these issues affecting residents.
